name: Hourly Rate Calculator
description: Industrial-grade hourly rate calculator incorporating labor, overhead, tooling, and quality costs per ISO 9001, Lean, and Six Sigma standards.

inputs.label: Direct Labor Hours per Unit
inputs.businessContext: Standard time per unit from time studies (ISO 9001 clause 7.1.4).

inputs.label: Average Hourly Wage
inputs.businessContext: Weighted average of direct labor wages including shift differentials.

inputs.label: Overhead Rate
inputs.businessContext: Includes facility, utilities, indirect labor, and depreciation (WERC DC Measures).

inputs.label: Tooling Cost per Unit
inputs.businessContext: Amortized cost of jigs, fixtures, and perishable tools (Lean TPM).

inputs.label: Quality Cost per Unit
inputs.businessContext: Cost of inspection, rework, scrap, and warranty (Six Sigma COPQ).

inputs.label: Batch Size
inputs.businessContext: Number of units produced per setup (Lean SMED).

inputs.label: Setup Time per Batch
inputs.businessContext: Time to change over equipment between batches (Lean SMED).

inputs.label: Target Profit Margin
inputs.businessContext: Desired markup over total cost (strategic pricing).

inputs.label: Currency
inputs.businessContext: ISO 4217 currency code for financial reporting.

inputs.label: Include Setup Cost in Rate
inputs.businessContext: If true, setup time is amortized across batch size.

rules.message: Batch size is very small; setup cost per unit may dominate. Consider increasing batch size or excluding setup cost.
Overhead rate exceeds 300% of low wages. Verify overhead allocation basis.
Quality cost exceeds 10% of direct labor cost. Investigate COPQ reduction opportunities.

thresholds.message: Direct labor cost per unit exceeds $100. Consider automation or process improvement.
Total cost per unit exceeds $500. Review value stream for waste reduction.

formulas.label: Direct Labor Cost per Unit
formulas.description: Base labor cost per unit from time study data.

formulas.label: Overhead Cost per Unit
formulas.description: Allocated overhead using traditional rate method.

formulas.label: Setup Cost per Unit
formulas.description: Amortized setup cost per unit based on batch size.

formulas.label: Machine Cost per Unit
formulas.description: Machine usage cost assuming machine runs concurrently with labor.

formulas.label: Total Cost per Unit
formulas.expression: direct_labor_cost + overhead_cost + tooling_cost_per_unit + quality_cost_per_unit + setup_cost_per_unit + machine_cost_per_unit
formulas.description: Sum of all cost components per unit.

formulas.label: Hourly Rate (Primary Result)
formulas.description: Effective hourly rate to cover all costs.

primary.label: Recommended Hourly Rate
primary.description: Final hourly rate covering all costs and profit margin.

hiddenLossDrivers.label: Excessive Setup Time
hiddenLossDrivers.description: Setup time > 10% of total batch processing time indicates SMED opportunity.

hiddenLossDrivers.label: High Quality Cost
hiddenLossDrivers.description: Quality cost > 15% of total cost suggests need for Six Sigma DMAIC.

hiddenLossDrivers.label: Overhead Bloat
hiddenLossDrivers.description: Overhead rate > 200% may indicate non-value-added activities.

suggestedActions.label: Reduce Setup Time
suggestedActions.description: Implement SMED techniques to reduce setup time by 50%.

suggestedActions.label: Improve Quality
suggestedActions.description: Deploy Six Sigma to reduce quality cost by 30%.

suggestedActions.label: Review Overhead Allocation
suggestedActions.description: Conduct activity-based costing to identify non-value-added overhead.

suggestedActions.label: Increase Batch Size
suggestedActions.description: Consolidate orders to reduce setup cost per unit.

dataConfidenceAdjusted.label: Data Confidence Score
dataConfidenceAdjusted.description: Adjusted confidence based on input variability and completeness (0-100%).
